pageTitle.value: Stephen Frears and Josué Mendez - Video

pageCaption.value: “For a Peruvian to work with [Stephen] Frears, it’s just not possible”, says young Lima film-maker Josué Mendez of the respected British director. But that’s exactly what happens when Frears chooses Mendez as his protégé and they share the “very precise art” of film-making, with Mendez directing his second full-length feature.

WaPageTitle.value: Stephen Frears and Josué Mendez - Video

description.value: Rolex Mentor and Protégé in Film, 2006 - 2007

text.value: “For a Peruvian to work with [Stephen] Frears, it’s just not possible”, says young Lima film-maker Josué Méndez of the respected British director. But that’s exactly what happens when Frears chooses Mendez as his protégé and they share the “very precise art” of film-making, with Mendez directing his second full-length feature.

pageTitle.value: Walter Murch and Sara Fgaier - Video

pageCaption.value: What does a film editor do? Walter Murch, editor of many iconic films, and his protégée, Italian film editor Sara Fgaier, provide succinct descriptions of the complex skill of constructing a feature film, the focus of their year-long mentorship.

WaPageTitle.value: Walter Murch and Sara Fgaier - Video

pageTitle.value: Zhang Yimou and Annemarie Jacir - Video

pageCaption.value: For Zhang Yimou, China’s creator of dazzling films, success is not about inspiration but the fruit “of hardships and efforts” as he meticulously masters every single scene. For his protégée Annemarie Jacir, working alongside him on set and in the editing room as he makes The Flowers of War, his supreme “visual sense” is the key to his rich and moving stories.

pageCaption.value: Emerging Filipino filmmaker Rafael Manuel collaborated for two years with acclaimed Chinese director Jia Zhang-Ke through the Rolex mentoring programme.

pageTitle.value: Tahar Ben Jelloun and Edem Awumey - Video

pageCaption.value: Moroccan-born and now one of France’s most fêted writers, Tahar Ben Jelloun declares that “literature is not reassuring, it’s disturbing”. Edem, from Togo, writing a novel of “dark nights and ghosts”, was the ideal protégé to accompany in the challenging process of creating fiction.

WaPageTitle.value: Tahar Ben Jelloun and Edem Awumey - Video

pageTitle.value: Pinchas Zukerman and David Aaron Carpenter - Video

pageCaption.value: World-renowned violinist and conductor Pinchas Zukerman says “the fiddle is synonymous with my existence”. He finds the same commitment in his protégé, young American violist David Aaron Carpenter, from the United States.

WaPageTitle.value: Pinchas Zukerman and David Aaron Carpenter - Video

pageTitle.value: Julie Taymor and Selina Cartmell - Video

pageCaption.value: Julie Taymor, famous for creating spectacular theatre, film and opera, wanted to mentor “someone I can have a dialogue with”. She found her in British director Selina Cartmell, for whom the mentorship is an ideal opportunity for the rare privilege of watching another director at work as Taymor creates a new opera, Grendel.
